hero.title: Discover Samarkand With Us
hero.subtitle: Certified guide Shohista — your gateway to the world of ancient architecture and Silk Road history

about.title: Meet Your Guide
about.subtitle: Certified specialist with deep knowledge of history
about.description: I'm a certified guide for Samarkand and Shahrisabz. I speak Russian, Uzbek, and English. I'll show you all the beauty of Samarkand — from the majestic Registan to the quiet alleyways of the old city. Every tour is a living history of the Great Silk Road.

items.question: What languages do you conduct tours in?
items.answer: I conduct tours in Russian, Uzbek, and English. We'll find the most comfortable communication option for each guest.

items.question: How do I book a tour?
items.answer: Leave a request through the form on the website, write to Instagram @shohista_guide, or contact us by email. We'll respond within 2 hours.

items.question: Is transport included in the price?
items.answer: Transport is already included in most of our tours. Clarify this when booking — we'll always find the optimal solution.

items.question: What is the minimum group size?
items.answer: The minimum group size is 1 person. We work with both individual tourists and groups of up to 10 people.

items.question: Can the route be changed?
items.answer: Of course! All routes are flexible and adapted to your interests. Tell us what you're interested in advance — we'll create the perfect tour.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: Payment is accepted in cash (USD or UZS) or by transfer. No hidden fees — the price you see is the final price.

items.question: Are there discounts for children?
items.answer: Yes! Children under 6 — free, from 6 to 12 years — 50% discount. Clarify details when booking.

items.question: How long is the overview tour of Samarkand?
items.answer: The standard overview tour of main attractions takes 4-5 hours. We can make it shorter or longer as you wish.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Uzbekistan?
items.answer: Citizens of CIS countries can enter Uzbekistan without a visa. For other countries, we recommend checking visa requirements on the official Uzbekistan MFA website.

items.question: When is the best time to visit Samarkand?
items.answer: The best time is spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). Comfortable temperatures and fewer tourists. But we work year-round!
